Library Cards Library Cards To register for a physical library card, please bring photo identification with proof of your current address to the Main Desk and fill out our registration form. Guardians of children ages 4 and older may register them for a library card with proof of address and a signature. Local students ages 11-18 may register with school identification or with a guardian.
If you do not have a current permanent address, you may get a local use Melrose Public Library card. 
If you are a 60+ year old Melrose resident and are homebound and unable to visit the library due to short or long-term disability or infirmity, please call and ask to speak with a circulation librarian regarding our homebound delivery application, 781-665-2313.
If you are a Massachusetts resident without a library card and would like to obtain an eCard, granting access to digital materials only, please apply online.
Please remember to bring your library card with you when you visit. You are responsible for all materials borrowed on your library card (and your dependents’ cards).   Your card may be used at all NOBLE (North Of Boston Library Exchange) libraries.
Using your physical library card, you are able to take out an unlimited number of physical materials. Digital materials are available for use with your library card number through various platforms accessible on our website.
Select Reference materials – 3 days and are not renewable
New books, magazines, and music CDs – 2 weeks
Older books, large print books, paperback books, audio books, Young Adult books, and most Children’s materials – 4 weeks
Items borrowed from another NOBLE library will follow the loan period of:
Printed materials – 2 weeks
Media materials – 1 week
Audio books – 2 weeks

Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits This pass provides admission at half price for op to 4 people (children under 12 months are admitted free).   
Visit the Boston Children’s Museum website to review museum visitor requirements and any updates to operating hours.  The library pass reservation calendar may not reflect changes to museum open dates and hours of operation.    
Reserve your Melrose Public Library pass for desired date.  You will receive a confirmation email with an  "Access Pass" link (if you do not have an email account, a printed confirmation may be picked up at the Main Desk of the library).
Donated by the Friends of Melrose Public Library.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass

Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Benefits This pass offers library cardholders the opportunity to purchase  ferry tickets for up to four people at a 50% discount, using a unique discount code. Ferries are boarded at Long Wharf North in Downtown Boston to Spectacle Island and Georges Island.  Please note, there is no direct service from Boston/Long Wharf to Peddocks Island.  All Peddocks Island departures will be from the Hingham Ferry Shipyard (some may choose to take the MBTA Ferry from Boston to Hingham first, in order to catch the ferry to Peddocks). 
Reserve your Melrose Public Library pass for desired date.  You will receive a confirmation email with an  "Access Pass" link which will include the discount code.  If you do not have an email account, a printed confirmation may be picked up at the Main Desk of the library.
    May 15 - June 14: Thursdays through Sundays
    June 16 - September 1: every day
    July 15 - September 1: every day
    September 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays
    September 26 - October 13: Fridays through Sundays
Peddocks Island from Hingham
    June 15 - September 7: every day BUT Tuesdays
    September 10 - October 12: Thursdays through Sundays
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "32 Quincy Street, Cambridge, MA 02138","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-harvard-art-museums_7_md.jpg","telephone": "617-495-9400","name": "Harvard Art Museums","url": "","description": "The Harvard Art Museums include three different institutions, all located near each other on the Cambridge campus of the university. The Fogg Museum&apos;s holdings include Western paintings, sculpture, decorative arts, photographs, prints, and drawings dating from the Middle Ages to the present. The Busch-Reisinger Museum contains Austrian Secession art, German expressionism, 1920s abstraction, and materials related to the Bauhaus, as well as late-medieval sculpture, eighteenth-century art, and noteworthy postwar and contemporary art from German-speaking Europe. The Arthur M. Sackler Museum is dedicated to works from Asia, the Middle East, and the Mediterranean."} Harvard Art Museums 32 Quincy Street, Cambridge, MA 02138617-495-9400http://www.harvardartmuseums.org/[Details/Reserve]
Harvard Art Museums The Harvard Art Museums include three different institutions, all located near each other on the Cambridge campus of the university. The Fogg Museum's holdings include Western paintings, sculpture, decorative arts, photographs, prints, and drawings dating from the Middle Ages to the present. The Busch-Reisinger Museum contains Austrian Secession art, German expressionism, 1920s abstraction, and materials related to the Bauhaus, as well as late-medieval sculpture, eighteenth-century art, and noteworthy postwar and contemporary art from German-speaking Europe. The Arthur M. Sackler Museum is dedicated to works from Asia, the Middle East, and the Mediterranean.
Categories: Art, Family, History
Pass Benefits Admission:
A pass in no longer required.
Admission is free to all visitors every day. Please check in at the Visitor Services desk upon arrival to allow us to share information about special exhibitions, gallery tours, programs, and other amenities available to ensure that you have a great visit!
